title: Field property
summary: Not an issue, but a suggestion: Working with BLToolkit I find myself having more properties with MapIgnore that actual DB fields. I would suggest a way to tag the DB fields, instead of tagging every other property with MapIgnore. If it doesn't add too much complexity there could ...
body_excerpt: Not an issue, but a suggestion: Working with BLToolkit I find myself having more properties with MapIgnore that actual DB fields. I would suggest a way to tag the DB fields, instead of tagging every other property with MapIgnore. If it doesn't add too much complexity there could even be a switch somewhere in DbManager. Thanks for your consideration and work on BLToolkit.

title: Introduce GetSchema() options (get / ignore SP's / progress / predefined SP set)
summary: It will be cool if GetSchema() behavior can be customized:
body_excerpt:
It will be cool if GetSchema() behavior can be customized:
- is disable to discover SP's schemas;
- define which exact SP's need to be processed;
- notify about progress.

Why?

For example one of my production database contains relatively many tables and about 10000 SP's. Usually i'm really need only very limited set of known SP's and and not all tables.
Many tables doesn't get too many problems, 'cause they processed relatively fast.
But grabbing schemas over 10000 SP's get about 8 minutes. In…

title: Linq2db (linq to sqlite) on .NET 4.0 ?
summary: My company requires our product to target .Net 4.0, the compiler complains lin2db needs System.Data.Services 4.0.0.0, which seems to require .Net 4.5. Is there any chance of getting this working? or maybe this is some problem with my setup? To help answer this questions, please I...
body_excerpt:
My company requires our product to target .Net 4.0, the compiler complains lin2db needs System.Data.Services 4.0.0.0, which seems to require .Net 4.5. Is there any chance of getting this working? or maybe this is some problem with my setup? To help answer this questions, please I would like to know if it's possible at all to work with linq2db on .NET 4.0 or if it was made depending heavily on 4.5. 

Best regards.
Marcelo.

title: why not use doubles instead of floats? (losing precision on Lat/Lon coordinate values)
summary: Hi, I've been trying to write my app using linq2db, I've noticed a quirk regarding REAL fields, being converted to floats on the generated data model. This can be easily avoided by doing a replace all "float" to "double" over the data model file. However, I was wondering if this ...
body_excerpt:
Hi, I've been trying to write my app using linq2db, I've noticed a quirk regarding REAL fields, being converted to floats on the generated data model. This can be easily avoided by doing a replace all "float" to "double" over the data model file. However, I was wondering if this is a wanted behavior, and if not, let you know about it. 

Thanks.
Marcelo.

It would be great if the following expression

``` C#
db.GetTable<Test>()
    .Take(1)

```

was translated into sql 

``` SQL
UPDATE TOP(1) [Test] SET [Name] = 'blah'
```

Now it cause no exception at run-time, but is translated into

UPDATE [Test] SET [Name] = 'blah'
```

So it seems like Take(1) call is simply ignored.

The reason of this query is to update first record in DB that meets some condition no matter in what order - to my mind it …

A postgres database often has multiple schemas.

When inserting data using linqtodb into a table with an auto-incrementing id, it correctly adds the schema to the sql.

```
nextval('schema1.table1_id_seq')
```

When trying to use InsertWithIdentity it fails due to not adding the schema:

```
SELECT currval('table1_id_seq')
```

Which causes you to get:
ERROR:  relation "table1_id_seq" does not exist at character 1…

How does startup performance compares to micro-frameworks? Is there a any background compilation step included?

title: Linq2SqlServer: SP param should be byte not sbyte
summary: When generating SP parameters, sbyte is used when byte would be better.
body_excerpt:
When generating SP parameters, sbyte is used when byte would be better.
A tinyint in SQL Server is not signed, so the .Net equalvalent should be a byte:
http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms187745.aspx

title: 1 to 1 mapping may be null
summary: We referencing a back reference in a 1 to 1 association, the back reference record may not exist, so the join should be a left join, not an inner join.  For example:
body_excerpt:
We referencing a back reference in a 1 to 1 association, the back reference record may not exist, so the join should be a left join, not an inner join.  For example:

``` c#
from p in cxt.Products
select new {
   p.ShoeDetail
}
```

Not all products have a ShoeDetail record (only shoes have a ShoeDetail record)

title: Contains generates invalid SQL for some types
summary: TimeSpan (mappeed to BIGINT, but also applies to default mapping to TIME ) and DateTimeOffset (mapped to datetimeoffset(7)) generate SQL like this:
body_excerpt:
TimeSpan (mappeed to BIGINT, but also applies to default mapping to TIME ) and DateTimeOffset (mapped to datetimeoffset(7)) generate SQL like this:
WHERE   [t1].[DateTime field] IN (4/24/8451 12:53:20 AM +00:00)
or
WHERE   [t1].[Time] IN (00:00:02.2222888)

Issue is in BasicSqlBuilder.BuildValue method which doesn't generate proper literals for DateTimeOffset and TimeSpan types.
It's an easy fix for DateTimeOffset, but for TimeSpan we need to know what kind of literal to generate: BIGNUM or TIME…

title: PostgreSQL composite primary key generate primary key attribute only on one column
summary: After model generation with `linq2db/t4models` only the first column of a composite primary key has the PrimaryKey attribute. It can be fixed manually by `GetColumn(tableName, column2Name).IsPrimaryKey = true;`, so it seems like a bug in `PostgreSQLSchemaProvider.GetPrimaryKeys` ...
body_excerpt:
After model generation with `linq2db/t4models` only the first column of a composite primary key has the PrimaryKey attribute. It can be fixed manually by `GetColumn(tableName, column2Name).IsPrimaryKey = true;`, so it seems like a bug in `PostgreSQLSchemaProvider.GetPrimaryKeys` rather than a bug in t4 templates. Suddenly I don't have enough experience with PostgreSQL to fix it myself quickly.
Thank you in advance.

title: database connection is not disposed in case of delayed execution
summary: Because the inner DataReader is using delayed execution (yield return), in some cases the session would not be created yet when the Dispose() of the DataConnection fires and thus not released afterwards.
body_excerpt:
Because the inner DataReader is using delayed execution (yield return), in some cases the session would not be created yet when the Dispose() of the DataConnection fires and thus not released afterwards.

Sample program:

title: Missing "check null" column in generated sql query
summary: Hello! I've found that two functiontionally identical linq queries produces different sql query:
body_excerpt:
Hello! I've found that two functiontionally identical linq queries produces different sql query:

"Ok" case:

``` C#
from root in context.GetTable<Root>()
join a in context.GetTable<ChildA>() on root.Id equals a.RootId into childs_a
join b in context.GetTable<ChildB>() on root.Id equals b.RootId into childs_b
select new
{
    Root = root,
    ChildA = childs_a.FirstOrDefault(),
    ChildB = childs_b.FirstOrDefault()
};
```

produces 

``` SQL
SELECT
    [root].[Id],

title: MS Access Long Integer field should be (long c#) and not (int c#)
summary: Hi,
body_excerpt:
Hi,

If we set a field [Long Integer] in MS Access the data type convert in the c# model is [int]. It should be [long] instead. MS Access [Long Integer] is 64 bits fields and (c# Int) is 32 bits data type.

This is a bit annoying as I have to store 32 bits number UNSIGNED and MS Access do not support unsigned type. So the only way for me is to store the data as 64 bits integer (Long Integer).

Best regards,

title: Fantastic performance!!
summary: Frans Bouma is working on an ORM benchmark. Until now it wasn't testing LINQ2DB:
body_excerpt:
Frans Bouma is working on an ORM benchmark. Until now it wasn't testing LINQ2DB:

https://github.com/FransBouma/RawDataAccessBencher

A few days ago I've added LINQ2DB to the test suite. Here's a preview of the next results:

http://pastebin.com/ZnaNswEE

As you can see LINQ2DB is the fastest ORM! In some cases it's faster than the hardcoded version. Thanks for your hard work!

I think this is caused by the method `CommandInfo.RebindParameters` which currently ignores return value parameters.

Pull request #367 solves this issue for me.

title: (Cannot ?) override column attributes at runtime
summary: If for some reason, you need to change the mapping for a table for one operation only, you can call something like `db.GetTable<ModelBase>().TableName('table1')`. However, there is no obvious way to do something like `db.GetTable<ModelBase>().ColumName(x => x.Id, 'idColumn1')`, l...
body_excerpt:
If for some reason, you need to change the mapping for a table for one operation only, you can call something like `db.GetTable<ModelBase>().TableName('table1')`. However, there is no obvious way to do something like `db.GetTable<ModelBase>().ColumName(x => x.Id, 'idColumn1')`, let alone override things like `IsPrimaryKey`.

I think an API similar to the one for tables would be nice, but in the meantime, is there any way to do the equivalent now?

title: Customizeable data providers
summary: What makes it hard to tweak existing data providers (to add support for custom logic for id generation or different sequence naming schemes or other small changes that do not justify a new data provider from scratch) is the fact that existing `ISqlBuilder` implementations are dec...
body_excerpt:
What makes it hard to tweak existing data providers (to add support for custom logic for id generation or different sequence naming schemes or other small changes that do not justify a new data provider from scratch) is the fact that existing `ISqlBuilder` implementations are declared as **internal**.

This requires workarounds like decorators which are getting really bloated if e.g. just a method like `BasicSqlBuilder.BuildGetIdentity` should be replaced.

title: sql query generated using nvarchar instead varchar
summary: We have sql server database where target table has float and varchar fields as PK, with its indexes...
body_excerpt:
We have sql server database where target table has float and varchar fields as PK, with its indexes...
When we query the table the sql statement generated by linq2db uses nvarchar comparison instead of varchar which causes serious performance issues as it does not use existing indexes.

example:

where row.CharProperty = 'A'

I have a connection string like <add name="PGSQL" connectionString="<deleted>" providerName="PostgreSQL" /> in the .config file. Then I pass the connection string name to the DataConnection(string) .ctor and get the NRE. Code examination showed that this is due to a swalled exception in the PostgeTools.ProviderDetector followed by return null;
I guess it would be better to do return _postgreSQLDataProvider; from the exception handler in this case.

You create a table with a field which is defined as VARCHAR(6). You insert two records to this table, and the values in that field are "Apple1" and "1Apple".

from record in table where record.field.Contains("Apple")

Linqtodb generates the following SQL:
WHERE record.field like @value1

The parameter ONLY allows 6 characters …

title: SqlCeSqlOptimizer with SqlTableType.Expression destroys table alias and causes NRE
summary: Using UpdatableQuery with SqlCe provider on any source that is a `SqlTableType.Expression` ( for ex.: `table.With("TABLOCK")`) causes table alias reset to `$` ... and finally a nullref exception when building the actual update expression where alias is null.
body_excerpt:
Using UpdatableQuery with SqlCe provider on any source that is a `SqlTableType.Expression` ( for ex.: `table.With("TABLOCK")`) causes table alias reset to `$` ... and finally a nullref exception when building the actual update expression where alias is null.

The root of the problem lies inside `SqlCeSqlOptimizer.Finalize()`
```cs
case QueryType.Update :
	selectQuery = GetAlternativeUpdate(selectQuery);
```

The selectQuery before this call contains the correct t1 alias, but at the end of `GetAl…

summary: There are two issues with the *CreateTable<T>* method.

There are two issues with the *CreateTable<T>* method.
1) The 4th parameter name *ownerName* is confusing. It should be named *schemaName*.
2) When an explicit schema name is set via the FluentMappingBuilder or via the ownerName parameter it is being respected only for the CREATE TABLE statement. But related CREATE SEQUENCE and CREATE TRIGGER statements have no schema specifications.

        /// <summary>Id</s…

title: Merge(): Precision is lost when converting DateTime objects (created from datetime2 in SQL Server) to SQL
summary: When I used the Merge() method on SqlServer 2012 recently I got a duplicate unique key exception. So I looked at the SQL generated and saw that DateTime2 values were truncated to 3 digits in the fractional seconds part (and that DateTime2 column was a part of the unique key).
body_excerpt:
When I used the Merge() method on SqlServer 2012 recently I got a duplicate unique key exception. So I looked at the SQL generated and saw that DateTime2 values were truncated to 3 digits in the fractional seconds part (and that DateTime2 column was a part of the unique key).
So, let's say we have the following datetime value:

``` c#
var dt  = DateTime.Parse("2010-12-14T05:00:07.4250141Z");
```

Merge() method will produce an SQL query containing '2010-12-14T00:00:07.425' for the datetime value…

title: Merge operation improvements
summary: It would be nice, if Merge operation will allow to specify what columns should be merged. Currently it just copy all updateable columns from source to target.
body_excerpt:
It would be nice, if Merge operation will allow to specify what columns should be merged. Currently it just copy all updateable columns from source to target.

Also (mentioned already in #234) it would be nice to have set expressions configurable, not just source to target copy.

title: Merge without insert
summary: Currently merge operation generates:
body_excerpt:
Currently merge operation generates:
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE
WHEN NOT MATCHED BY Target THEN INSERT
WHEN NOT MATCHED BY Source THEN DELETE
delete operation could be enabled or disabled. Could you add and option to disable insert operations too?

title: Custom merge predicates support
summary: Currently merge operation use only primary keys match predicate.
body_excerpt: Currently merge operation use only primary keys match predicate.

title: More freedom on merge actions configuration
summary: Currently merge supports
body_excerpt:
Currently merge supports
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE
WHEN NOT MATCHED BY TARGET THEN INSERT
WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE

It will be more usefull, if type of operation could be specified by user.
E.g. in combination with #336 I would like to perform delete of matched records (by primary key and additional field, added as extra predicate).
Another example:
delete by match on composite primary key (no custom predicates required)

summary: We have performed some investigation on linq2db's GetQuery and FindQuery methods and found following interesting moment.
body_excerpt:
We have performed some investigation on linq2db's GetQuery and FindQuery methods and found following interesting moment.

GetQuery uses FindQuery with double-checked locking pattern to prevent acquiring locks when compiled expression already found in cache and returned by the first FindQuery call: http://take.ms/irSj2 

But in the FindQuery there is also lock region which uses the same lock object as GetQuery: http://take.ms/QPkVZ

This lock region negates double-checked locking pattern effect i…

title: [Feature] Support full set of joins
summary: As from this documentation: https://www.w3schools.com/sql/sql_join.asp
body_excerpt:
As from this documentation: https://www.w3schools.com/sql/sql_join.asp
There are four main types of joins:

Join Type| linq2db||
----|----|--
**INNER JOIN**|Fully|Fully supported
**LEFT JOIN**|Fully|Fully supported
**RIGHT JOIN**||There is workaround: swap queries and make left join :)
**FULL OUTER JOIN**||No simple way to define

I propose to support something like this (like with DefaultIfEmpty):
```cs
var q = from t1 in Table1
        from t2 in Table2.Where(t1.Key == t2.Key || t1.Key2 != t2.…

https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/78e0f9d188f5a19ae596fff2dc74f579d05152da/Source/Linq/Builder/SubQueryContext.cs#L150-L161

https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/78e0f9d188f5a19ae596fff2dc74f579d05152da/Source/Linq/Builder/JoinBuilder.cs#L44-L55

However, `ParameterExpression.Name` could be null.

title: Increment value in row with one query
summary: Question: how can I increment one value in one row with one query?
body_excerpt:
Question: how can I increment one value in one row with one query?
Let's say I have following table:
`create table Orders
(
  OrderId int not null,
  Quantity int not null
);
insert into Orders values (1, 0);`

In SQL I can make query `UPDATE Orders SET Quantity = Quantity + 1 WHERE OrderId = 1;`

How can I achieve that using linq2db?
`db.Orders
    .Where(o => o.OrderId == 1)
    .Set(o => o.Quantity, /* o.Quantity + 1  -- o does not exist in current context */)
    .Update();`

title: Adding exrta where clauses based on entity attributes or interfaces
summary: In Nhibernate, one can extend the BaseClassMap in order to introduce custom SQL to be included with the queries generated by overriding the default where clauses generation.  Is it possible to do so using Linq2DB?
body_excerpt:
In Nhibernate, one can extend the BaseClassMap in order to introduce custom SQL to be included with the queries generated by overriding the default where clauses generation.  Is it possible to do so using Linq2DB?

title: CreateTable: SQLite needs Integer—not BigInt—for AUTOINCREMENT fields
summary: SQL generated by `CreateTable()` can look something like this:
body_excerpt:
SQL generated by `CreateTable()` can look something like this:

> CREATE TABLE [FailTable]( [id] **BigInt** PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT )

which SQLite considers invalid and produces this error:

> AUTOINCREMENT is only allowed on an **INTEGER** PRIMARY KEY

SQLite does not actually have **BigInt** type, instead automatically replacing it with **Integer** via its "[type affinity](https://sqlite.org/datatype3.html)" system. However, for some reason, ~~(possibly a bug in SQLite)~~ SQLite has [decide…

var name = "Some Category";

evaluates to

```
-- DataContext MySql
DECLARE @p1 VarChar(13) -- String
SET     @p1 = 'some category'

SELECT
	`t1`.`Id`,
	`t1`.`Name`
FROM
	`categories` `t1`
WHERE
	Lower(`t1`.`Name`) = @p1
```

title: Firebird ADO.NET-Provider connection pool error on connection lost
summary: First, this is not a linq2DB error, it is a tip for a workaround caused by the current Firebird ADO.NET provider (Version 4.6.0.0 - 5.12.1.0)
body_excerpt:
First, this is not a linq2DB error, it is a tip for a workaround caused by the current Firebird ADO.NET provider (Version 4.6.0.0 - 5.12.1.0)
If you are working with connection pools and your connection is lost e.g. because the Firebird server is closed, the pooled connection is still marked as 'opened'. So every database access will create an Exception (FBException) 'Error reading data from the connection'. So if the Firebird server is accessible again you still cannot access it because the old…

title: Repeated construction of context using FluentMappingBuilder gets slower each time

body_excerpt:
### Issue
Repeatedly creating context classes when some attributes are set with the FluentMappingBuilder causes each construction to get slower. For a simple database, like the one in my example, it not very noticeable. However, for a complex database, the growth is much faster. I am using linq2db in a long running process that can run for days which makes this issue significant.

#### Workaround
To work around this, I just use a static boolean to tell me if the context has already been initiali…

title: LinqToDB.Sql.Between handles struct `null` values incorrectly / counter-intuitively
summary: In my view the current implementation for nullable structs handles null values incorrectly (or at least counter-intuitively). null values should be completely ignored instead of actually compared. For example, `query.Where(x => x.Number.Between<int>(1, null))` should be evaluated...
body_excerpt:
In my view the current implementation for nullable structs handles null values incorrectly (or at least counter-intuitively). null values should be completely ignored instead of actually compared. For example, `query.Where(x => x.Number.Between<int>(1, null))` should be evaluated as `Number >= 1` not as `Number between 1 and NULL` which can never evaluate to `true`.

A query like `query.Where(x => x.Number.Between<int>(null, null))` should be ignored altogether.

Current implementation:
https://…

and it would look like:

selct * 
from (select * from table where condition1) q1 
full  join (select * from table) q2 on condition1 & confition3
```

while should be

selct * 
from (select * from table where condition1) q1

Sealed: `LinqToDB.Mapping.PrimaryKeyAttribute`, `LinqToDB.Mapping.NotNullAttribute`

The problem is that `OnConnectionTypeCreated` is never called in this case: it is called only in `DynamicDataProviderBase.GetConnectionType()` which is called only from the function `DynamicDataProviderBase.IsCompatibleConnection()`.

```sql

"t1"."MANDT",

FROM
"_SYS_BIC"."prd.global.ecc/CV_MARA" "t1"
LIMIT 100
```

Actual:
```sql
-- SapHana
SELECT
"t1"."MANDT",
....
FROM
"_SYS_BIC"."prd"."global"."ecc/CV_MARA" "t1"

body_excerpt:

Seems that linq2db caches such embedded lambda expressions too aggressively and reuse first expression in subsequent queries. Clearing cache with `LinqToDB.Linq.Query.ClearCaches()` solves problem.

title: [Idea] FodyAddin for linq2db
summary: What are your thoughts about the idea of ​​building an addin of fody for linq2db?
body_excerpt:
What are your thoughts about the idea of ​​building an addin of fody for linq2db?

An Addin of Fody (https://github.com/Fody/Fody) can modify the IL-Code at compile time. 

For example, you could automatic create CompiledQuery at compile time. This improves the performance of linq2db and the developer does not have to write it himself.

title: Documentation: how to do group by
summary: Currently there's no documentation on how to do group by.  I know the following works
body_excerpt:
Currently there's no documentation on how to do group by.  I know the following works
```C#
from product in db.Product
from category in db.Category.InnerJoin(c => c.ID = product.CategoryID )
group product by new {product.CategoryID, category} into _
select new {
    categoryCost = product.Sum(p => p.UnitPrice),
    _.Key.CategoryID,
    _.Key.category.Name
}
```

I'd love to get this documented for others, and learn other ways that linq2db supports group by.

title: Add async transaction methods
summary: For 2.6.3 release we will add BeginTransactionAsync method, which will just open connection in async manner, if it is not opened, because ADO.NET doesn't have API for Begin/Commit/Rollback Transaction methods.
body_excerpt:
For 2.6.3 release we will add BeginTransactionAsync method, which will just open connection in async manner, if it is not opened, because ADO.NET doesn't have API for Begin/Commit/Rollback Transaction methods.

For next release we should try to improve this situation and implement async versions of those API for providers, that support it (at least mysqlconnector and npgsql).

Related issues with more information:
Async transaction API request for .net core: https://github.com/dotnet/corefx/issu…

title: Store dynamic columns outside of current entity
summary: Hello,
body_excerpt:
Hello,
is it possible to store the dynamic columns to another dictionary <string, object> that doesn't exist in the entity itself? Our problem is that we have to use a given datastructure but have tables, that differ for each tenant. So we cannot extend the model class with a dictionary and the attribute [DynamicColumnsStore]. title: EXISTS quieries break in i series provider in 2.8

body_excerpt:
As @RoyC-Retail-Assist has no access to an IBM i series any more we are cooperating with him to maintain the i series provider. Upgrading to 2.8 has caused EXISTS queries to break.

We narrowed the problem down to linq2db ConvertInternal in QueryVisitor.cs line 1483.

The problem is that "Exists" is an SqlFunction wrapped inside a "FuncLikePredicate" construct. The framework unwraps it and passes on the inner SqlFunction to the optimizer expecting to get an SqlFunction in return. As the i series…

title: Deprecate netstandard 1.6
summary: Currently we investigate options to remove netstandard 1.6 support from linq2db 3.0 (which we expect to release in first half of 2019).
body_excerpt:
Currently we investigate options to remove netstandard 1.6 support from linq2db 3.0 (which we expect to release in first half of 2019).
Effectively it means we stop support for .net core 1.1 and old xamarin versions (anything else?).

If anyobody have any objections regarding this move, feel free to comment this issue.

title: Introduce MERGE into internal query AST
summary: Merge operation must became a first-class member of our query AST.
body_excerpt:
Merge operation must became a first-class member of our query AST.

Right now we use MERGE operation for InsertOr*, Merge API and legacy Merge API, which are separate implementations of MERGE command with different level of support per provider.

Moving it to AST will allow us:
- use single codebase
- integrate Merge with other features like CTE and OUTPUT

title: LinqService sends same query AST for providers with different sql flags
summary: Discovered by #1436
body_excerpt:
Discovered by #1436

For postgresql 9.2 and 9.3 providers we generate some queries differently compared to 9.5+ due to lack of sql features by those versions of postgresql. For some reason when we execute same test in single session with both 9.2/9.3 provider and 9.5+ provider -  we send cached query ast from first linq test for all linq tests with other providers.

So if pgsql 9.5 linq test executed first, pgsql 9.2 test will fail due to use of unsupported sql features.

title: Median's PartitionBy does not convert to SQL correctly
summary: Linq2Db: 3.0.0-RC.0
body_excerpt:
Linq2Db: 3.0.0-RC.0
DB: Oracle

The converted Median part to SQL of 
```
.Select(x => new Bla
{
   Data = new Data {F1 = Sql.Ext.Median(x.Data.F1).Over().PartitionBy(x.Other.Id).ToValue()}
}
```

is

`MEDIAN(x_10.F1) OVER(PARTITION BY x_10.F1)`

So x.Other.Id is ignored. Instead the parameter of Median is used for partition.

title: Incorrect order of serverName, databaseName, schemaName parameters in DataExtensions.cs
summary: When I call
body_excerpt:
When I call 
`db.InsertWithIdentityAsync(entity, schemaName: schema.Name)`

I get exception about my PostgreSQL table does not exist.

I discovered that my `schemaName` value actually are set to `serverName`.

It's because of incorrect order of `serverName, databaseName, schemaName` parameters in [DataExtensions.cs](https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/DataExtensions.cs)

Using named parameters prevents such kind of bugs.

title: Bug: invalid removal of unused aggregation
summary: We have found a case where linq2db simplifies a query too aggressively and produces an incorrect result.
body_excerpt:
We have found a case where linq2db simplifies a query too aggressively and produces an incorrect result.

Our high-level use case is that we produce a paginated list.
To do this we construct a complex query, which is then executed twice: 
- `.Count()` to have the total number of rows
- `.Skip().Take().ToList()` to fetch one page of data

During the `.Count()` execution, no field is selected, which leads to massive simplifications made by linq2db.
From a perf perspective this is very nice, unfort…

I've noticed that if a MappingSchema is passed into DataConnection, we new up another MappingSchema (to integrate the DataProvider's Mapping Schema).

In one of my codebases, using `Clone` on an existing DataConnection showed a marked improvement in benchmark results (as, among other things, it avoided re-creating the merged Schema).

`Clone` works for the moment but I'm not sure if it's robust for all scenarios one may encouter.

title: [PostgreSQL] The Select clause must contain column definitions when a query with the GROUP BY
summary: Hi!
body_excerpt:
Hi!
When we use a subquery with GROUP BY and JOIN clauses together column definitions in SELECT clause replace with `*`.

```SQL
-- PostgreSQL PostgreSQL.9.5 PostgreSQL
SELECT
	pa."Id"
FROM
	"ProductAttributeMapping" pam
		INNER JOIN "ProductAttributeTable" pa ON pam."ProductAttributeId" = pa."Id"
WHERE
	EXISTS(
		SELECT
			*
		FROM
			"ProductTable" p
				INNER JOIN "ProductAttributeMapping" pam_1 ON p."Id" = pam_1."ProductId"
		WHERE
			p."Id" >= pam."ProductId"
		GROUP BY
			p."Id"
		HAVING
	…

title: MiniProfiler.GetProviderSpecificFieldType could hide real column data type
summary: Discovered during refactoring in https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/pull/2455.
body_excerpt:
Discovered during refactoring in https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/pull/2455.
For oracle timestamptz it returns `DateTime` type instead of oracle-specific type for `DateTimeOffset` mapping, which leads to read of value with incorrect offset.
We should try (if possible) to fix DateTime mapping and properly resolve `GetProviderSpecificFieldType` call

title: ExecuteReaderAsync should use CommandBehavior

body_excerpt:
The question is well explained here:

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/28543293/entity-framework-async-operation-takes-ten-times-as-long-to-complete/

Linq2db is affected as well. So it has terrible performance with byte[] columns (varbinary(Max)).

CommandBehavior.SequentialAccess increase speed maybe in ten times.

title: Linq StartsWith() translate to "like" in sql query (Firebird)
summary: Linq StartsWith() translate to "like" in sql query(Firebird)
body_excerpt:
Linq StartsWith() translate to "like" in sql query(Firebird)
for example:
`from c in db.GetTable<MAN>() where c.Name.StartsWith(txt) select c.Name`
linq2db translate it to:
`select name from man where name like "txt" escape "~"`
but I think should be:
`select name from man where name **starting with** "txt"`

title: Simplify queries with known cardinality
summary: This is a follow-up of #2478
body_excerpt:
This is a follow-up of #2478 

In that issue, an incorrect optimization was fixed by not totally removing aggregates from subqueries that don't have GROUP BY, as this would change their cardinality (from always 1 to 0 when there's no row in the recordset).

This issue is a potential further optimization: such a subquery can be completely removed.

Specifically:
1. An aggregated query that has no GROUP BY _always_ has cardinality 1, no matter how many rows are in the result set before aggregation…

In cases when the collation of the catalog (`CATALOG_COLLATION` option of `CREATE DATABASE`) is different than the default collation of the database, the collation of the database name (catalog) is different than the collation of the names of the schema, tables, etc. The name of the database use the default database collation while the names of the objects in the database use the collation specified by `CATALOG_COLLATION`.

One reason for having different collations is to use a case-sensitive co…

Jetbrains Rider reports errors in the templates after installation.
As the developer of the Rider T4 plugin, I think that this is the intended behaviour.
These templates are red in Rider because they are not self-sufficient: some templates contain references to libraries, while others contain usages of symbols from those libraries. It is absolutely impossible to resolve those symbols before both templates with assembly references and templates with usages of their symbols are included into a use…

summary: Currently, when you call `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.Delete` or `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.Update`, you get back the number of items affected.
body_excerpt:
Currently, when you call `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.Delete` or `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.Update`, you get back the number of items affected.

It would be nice to optionally be able to get back the items that were deleted or updated. In SQL Server, for example, this can be done with the [OUTPUT](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/queries/output-clause-transact-sql) clause.

A couple of ways the API could change to support this:
 * Add overloads with an `out` parameter
 * Have a newly-named m…

summary: this is related to issue #345.   The connection string for Postgresql does not work if "Protocol=3;" is included in the connection string in app.config.
body_excerpt:
this is related to issue #345.   The connection string for Postgresql does not work if "Protocol=3;" is included in the connection string in app.config.

The instructions in CopyMe.PostgreSQL.tt.txt includes the "Protocol=3;"  and should be removed.   

Note: the "Protocol=3;"  part worked in  version 1.0.2, which is referenced by the sample project https://github.com/linq2db/examples/tree/master/PostgreSQL/GetStarted, which might benefit from updating ;)

title: Incorrect Query generation for Ternary
summary: SQL Server; l2db 3.2.3. Introduced after 3.1.6
body_excerpt:
SQL Server; l2db 3.2.3. Introduced after 3.1.6

Table and column are not important, only that the column is numeric (int, tinyint, etc.)
```csharp
context.Equipment.Select(e => Sql.AsSql(Sql.Between(e.EquipmentTypeId, 2, 5) ? 0 : 1)).ToList();
```
Renders to the following SQL:
```sql
SELECT
	IIF([e].[EquipmentTypeId] BETWEEN 2 AND 5 = 1, 0, 1)
FROM
	[dbo].[Equipment] [e]
```

There is a false comparison (`=1`) when the value is already a bool expression, so the syntax doesn't work right.

title: can't load table columns for DB2
summary: seems there is a bug in DB2LUWSchemaProvider class, when loading columns of table, it didn't trim the tailing space of the schema name, causes linq query didn't return any column.
body_excerpt:
seems there is a bug in DB2LUWSchemaProvider class, when loading columns of table, it didn't trim the tailing space of the schema name, causes linq query didn't return any column.

the problem is in the GetColumns method here:
```c#
var tableId     = dataConnection.Connection.Database + "." + rd.GetString(0) + "." + rd.GetString(1);
```
and it should be:
```c#
var tableId     = dataConnection.Connection.Database + "." + rd.ToString(0) + "." + rd.ToString(1);
```

The problem is that `DataContext`'s Dispose method is private and `IDisposable` is implemented explicitly, so I can't override it, nor can I implement `IDisposable` and call it.

A simple solution will be to make it `virtual` so inherited classes can override it if needed.

title: TempTable.Dispose can throw an exception
summary: `TempTable.Dispose` can mask exceptions by the nature of the fact that a failed SQL statement can rollback a transaction, and the `Dispose` method will try to drop a table that does not exist anymore.
body_excerpt:
### Problem

`TempTable.Dispose` can mask exceptions by the nature of the fact that a failed SQL statement can rollback a transaction, and the `Dispose` method will try to drop a table that does not exist anymore.

Consider the following sample:

```cs
await using var dc = GetDataConnection();
await using var transaction = await dc.BeginTransactionAsync(cancellationToken);
await using var table = await dc.CreateTempTableAsync("#Temp_Products", productsToUpdate, cancellationToken: cancellationTok…

title: TraceInfo: ExecuteNonQuery vs ExecuteReader difference
summary: For trace events, there is no way to tell ExecuteNonQuery from ExecuteReader or ExecuteScalar.
body_excerpt:
For trace events, there is no way to tell ExecuteNonQuery from ExecuteReader or ExecuteScalar.
ExecuteNonQuery method produces BeforeExecute and AfterExecute trace steps, but ExecuteReader
has an additional Completed step which can be used to measure fetch time.

Consider an application that is able to log its SQL statements. Each statement should appear only once.
For ExecuteNonQuery commands the application displays the start time and execution time.
For ExecuteReader commands the application …

title: Feature - query tag (TagWith)
summary: There is one helpful feature in ef core: TagWith. It adds custom tag as a comment before SELECT query. This feature can be very helpful to identify the heaviest queries in a database, because such tag will be stored together with query text in the database query plan cache.
body_excerpt:
There is one helpful feature in ef core: TagWith. It adds custom tag as a comment before SELECT query. This feature can be very helpful to identify the heaviest queries in a database, because such tag will be stored together with query text in the database query plan cache.

I haven't found anything similar in linq2db and I think it would be great to implement this here as well.

title: [PostreSQL] Read/write infinite double precision values
summary: Can not insert an infinite (plus/minus) value into a double precision column in a postgres table (linq2db 3.3.0).
body_excerpt:
Can not insert an infinite (plus/minus) value into a double precision column in a postgres table (linq2db 3.3.0).
Seems linq2db don't quote those values according to the postgres documentation.

Output sql with infinite value for an insert query looks like
```sql
insert into some_table 
  (id, value)
values
  (1, Infinite),
  (2, -Infinite);
```

But must be
```sql
insert into some_table 
  (id, value)
values
  (1, '+Infinite'),
  (2, '-Infinite');
```

Npgsql driver supports this feature https:…

body_excerpt:
Most database operations go through steps like BeforeExecute → AfterExecute → Completed (or Error). That's very convenient because it allows to gather statistics along the way and then log it once per query.

For ExecuteReader we can measure query execution (the time between BeforeExecute and AfterExecute) and data fetching (between AfterExecute and Completed) separately. That's so cool.

Unfortunately, some queries don't trigger the **Completed** step. Examples:

User-named PRIMARY KEY constraint `PK_XXX` is not allowed on temp table `#XXX` because it is being created in a contained database. <...>

title: Announcement: Drop SQL Server 2000 support in v4
summary: We plan to drop SQL Server 2000 support from linq2db in next major version (v4).
body_excerpt:
We plan to drop SQL Server 2000 support from linq2db in next major version (v4).

1. There is no support for it already in .net core (it uses different protocol which wasn't ported to .net core)
2. We don't perform regular testing for it

If anyone interested in SQL Server 2000 long-term support, we could extract it to separate external project.

title: WHERE on boolean field when there's no boolean dbtype
summary: I encountered a bad SQL translation with Oracle.
body_excerpt:
I encountered a bad SQL translation with Oracle.

My Linq query uses some `ExpressionMethod`, but basically it comes down to this:
(`Flag` is a `bool` property)
```cs
from s in db.SomeTable
where db.OtherTable.Single(o => o.Id == tcId).Flag
select s
```

title: Don't select unnecessary columns
summary: ```cs
body_excerpt:
```cs
bool b = false;
var q = from t in db.Tbl1
        select new
        {
            t.C1,
            Other = b ? new { t.C2, t.C3, t.C4 } : null
        };
```
The SQL executed is:
```sql
SELECT
        [t].[C1],
        [t].[C2],
        [t].[C3],
        [t].[C4]
FROM
        [Tbl1] [t]
```
The property `Other` in the results is null. But it loads the columns C2, C3 and C4 from the DB.
Desired behavior: don't load columns which aren't needed.

title: Add net6/C# 10 support
summary: - enable C# 10 (we need it at least for `record struct` support testing)
body_excerpt:
- enable C# 10 (we need it at least for `record struct` support testing)
- add net6 target to tests and CI
- add net6 target (build-only, no release) to `linq2db` to detect nullability issues with recent NRT annotations

Regarding last one - currently don't see a reason to release `net6` version as we don't use anything from it. Could be added when we decide to add built-in `DateOnly`/`TimeOnly` mappings.

Related issues:
- #2977
- #3357

title: PostgreSQL 'partial' locking hint on JOIN
summary: As many other RDBMS PostgreSQL supports fine-grained locks. This is made with locking hints on query (which is also very similar to other database systems) like:
body_excerpt:
As many other RDBMS PostgreSQL supports fine-grained locks. This is made with locking hints on query (which is also very similar to other database systems) like:
```
SELECT* 
FROM USER
WHERE id = 1 FOR NO KEY UPDATE;
```
tells that we are going to update some field(s) (except id) and the hint "FOR NO KEY UPDATE" will cause other writers of user with id = 1 to wait for the end of the current transaction.

As far as I understand concerning linq2db that hint could be added right before the actual q…

title: Bad typing of LEAD and LAG default values
summary: The `default` parameter of LEAD and LAG analytical functions is defined as `int`.
body_excerpt:
The `default` parameter of LEAD and LAG analytical functions is defined as `int`.
This is incorrect, as the `default` parameter should have the same generic type `T` as the main expression.

https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/8200bce088a9001bf794e920ed27ac4c9835b6fa/Source/LinqToDB/Sql/Sql.Analytic.cs#L717-L745

 This was unnoticed because the unit tests in `AnalyticTests.cs` all use int expressions...

title: OUTPUT: PostgreSQL support (RETURNING  clause)
summary: Using postgres I use "returning *" when inserting or updating data.
body_excerpt:
Using postgres I use "returning *" when inserting or updating data. 
This allows you to get not only the primary key but also all the fields. 
Is it possible to do this with linq2db?

 I think in some cases this is more convenient than inserting data and then selecting them. 
If you use "returning *", you get an atomic operation and you do not need to select the primary key type.

Something like
```csharp
T obj = db.Insert<T>(T item);
T obj = db.Update<T>(T item);
```

title: BulkCopyType.MultipleRows could insert incorrect data into varchar columns in Firebird
summary: Discovered by #2829
body_excerpt:
Discovered by #2829

`MultipleRows` use sql like:
```sql
insert from (
select {columns} from rdb$database
union all
select {columns} from rdb$database
...
union all
select {columns} from rdb$database
)
```

If one of target columns is of varchar type it could result in column values padded with spaces due to following reasons:
- we specify column value as string literal which is typed as CHAR(literal_length)
- firebird converts subquery column type to CHAR(max_literal_len) (basically padding sma…

db.GetCte<record>(cte => query1.Concat(cte_query1).Concat(cte_query2))
```

produce multiple nested subqueries instead of plain `UNION ALL` of all parts (more concats - more subqueries nesting):

```sql
WITH cte() AS
(
    SELECT ... FROM (
        query1
        UNION ALL
        cte_query1
    )
    UNION ALL
    cte_query2
)
```


instead of 
```sql
WITH cte() AS
(
    query1
    UNION ALL
    cte_query1
    UNION ALL
    cte_query2
)
```

title: Implement Merge into updatable query or CTE
summary: The current merge API accepts only `ITable<TTarget>` as a merge target.  In Microsoft SQL Server at least it is valid to have any updatable query or CTE as the target of a merge statement.
body_excerpt:
The current merge API accepts only `ITable<TTarget>` as a merge target.  In Microsoft SQL Server at least it is valid to have any updatable query or CTE as the target of a merge statement.

Specifically this is useful when updating certain types of history tables or slowly-changing dimensions (Type 2 specifically) in data warehouse environments.  In the case of Type2 SCDs the merge target is the subset of rows that are currently active.  All of the terms of the merge act on the selected subset o…

title: Hide types from `Sql` static class
summary: For 4.0, I propose the following breaking change:
body_excerpt:
For 4.0, I propose the following breaking change:

Nest the types from `Sql` (lines 284-413 below) into a public nested class `Types`.
https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/Sql/Sql.cs#L284-L413

The goal is to make it easier to `using static LinqToDb.Sql`.
The problem today is that when we statically import this class, member `DateTime()` conflicts with `using System`, so we can't use stuff like `DateTime.Today` in the query without prefixing to lift ambiguity.

title: Add data reader completed interceptor
summary: `QueryProc` case is more tricky. It calls `ExecuteReader[Async]` and main issue here is that output parameters (at least for SQL Server, cannot tell about other providers right now, but probably the same) available only after all data consumed from reader. E.g. this is the reason...
body_excerpt:
`QueryProc` case is more tricky. It calls `ExecuteReader[Async]` and main issue here is that output parameters (at least for SQL Server, cannot tell about other providers right now, but probably the same) available only after all data consumed from reader. E.g. this is the reason why we generate `ToList` calls in scaffolded context for procedures with output parameters.

I don't see currently how you can handle it with interceptors, probably we need another interceptor to trigger after data read…

title: Regression in Oracle array-bound BulkCopy
summary: After updating to latest 4.1.1 (from 3.x), we have a huge regression in BulkCopy when using the proprietary Oracle strategy using array-bound parameters.
body_excerpt:
After updating to latest 4.1.1 (from 3.x), we have a huge regression in BulkCopy when using the proprietary Oracle strategy using array-bound parameters.

All queries on the same `DataConnection` after `BulkCopy` fail with an `InvalidCastException`, that can't convert a random type (int32, string, DateTime) to Array.

It seems linq2db tries to re-use `DbCommand` objects, but it doesn't reset them properly.
Specifically, after the bulk copy, the `ArrayBindCount` property is not set back to 0.
So …

public static string Collate(this string expr, [SqlQueryDependent] string collation)

It should take and return a nullable `string?` with an annotation to say it returns non-null string if `expr` is non-null.

title: [CLI] Custom code generation
summary: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/discussions/3680
body_excerpt:
https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/discussions/3680

Currently we don't support 3rd-party low-level customization of code-generation process.

There is an API to apply modifications to generated code model, e.g. we use it to modify equality operators for some types, see [SqlBoolEqualityConverter](https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB.Tools/CodeModel/Visitors/Custom/SqlBoolEqualityConverter.cs). But:
- this API is not exposed to CLI users
- AST API requires review to make…

title: Procedures schema suport for Informix
summary: Recently we closed last gaps in this functionality by adding procedures support to schema provider for MySql and PostgreSQL. The only remaining provider that miss this functionality (except databases that doesn't support user-defined procedures) is Informix provider.
body_excerpt:
Recently we closed last gaps in this functionality by adding procedures support to schema provider for MySql and PostgreSQL. The only remaining provider that miss this functionality (except databases that doesn't support user-defined procedures) is Informix provider.

For reference implementation you can check those PRs:
- MySQL: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/pull/993
- PostgreSQL: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/pull/1184

title: IDataContext.CreateTable lacks

body_excerpt:
CreateTable puts identity column to the last position in create table script, so when we use, for example, sql server management studio or other manager, primary key column appeared in the end of the column list, when most people expect it to appear as first.
The Second problem is that createtable does not generate foreign key statements, even if all required data and relations described in a mapping class.

body_excerpt:
My CTE query returns a concatenated string 'Label' that contains the parent's label + the current label, e.g. "parentfolder/currentfolder". This works well on SQLite, but not on (MS)SQL. There I get the following error: 

```
Types don't match between the anchor and the recursive part in column "Label" of recursive query "CTE".
```

MsSQL notices that 'Label' is once a NVARCHAR(50) and once a NVARCHAR(MAX). Typical solution in SQL is to cast both columns to the same type (e.g. CAST(Label AS NVAR…

title: Decouple model attributes from model metadata
summary: It's hacky because of a leaky abstraction: marking a property by an attribute to specify it as a column - is an implementation detail and it's quite low-level. If we need to say 'this property is a column' in a generic way - there must be a higher-level entity/API of doing so (wi...
body_excerpt:
It's hacky because of a leaky abstraction: marking a property by an attribute to specify it as a column - is an implementation detail and it's quite low-level. If we need to say 'this property is a column' in a generic way - there must be a higher-level entity/API of doing so (without using attributes).
Think it the other way - IMetadataReader returns an attribute which is really not there (not on the class type/property type)! And returning this 'virtual' attribute is nothing but a workaround (…

**Current usage of `ILinqToDBSettings`**
I use `ILinqToDBSettings` as easy way to inject multiple connection strings (as described here https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db#net-core) from top-level project and switch them dynamically. This way I don't need to pass a particular connection string all way down per each query because all strings are stored internally by `linq2db`.

title: Bad SQL generated for INSERT w/ CTE + JOIN
summary: PR with failing test case: #3946
body_excerpt:
PR with failing test case: #3946

The issue is that when doing an INSERT from a query that contains a CTE + a JOIN, linq2db wraps the query in SELECT *.
Oracle doesn't like that SELECT * and fails with ORA-00918, Column ambiguously defined (which makes no sense to me, but whatever).
That SELECT * serves no purpose and can be removed, the query then executes successfully.

Generated SQL structure:
```sql
INSERT INTO ...
SELECT * FROM (  -- This confuses Oracle
  WITH cte (..)
  SELECT ..
  FROM c…

title: [V5]Lower dependency versions for microsoft abstraction libs
summary: Linq2db uses DependencyInjection and Logging abstractions from the MS extensions namespace. It is generally not the best practive to aggresively upgrade those packages in library projects as they are pretty much backwards compatible back to v1 and might create conflicts to consum...
body_excerpt: Linq2db uses DependencyInjection and Logging abstractions from the MS extensions namespace. It is generally not the best practive to aggresively upgrade those packages in library projects as they are pretty much backwards compatible back to v1 and might create conflicts to consumer projects. Linq2db only uses IServiceCollection and ILoggerFactory / ILogger , you can stay to a very early version of abstractions for both or at least stick to v6.

title: MappingSchema.EntityDescriptorCreatedCallback should be static
summary: Right now MappingSchema.EntityDescriptorCreatedCallback is defined per-MappingSchema instance, which makes it non-trivial to configure, because user should attach callback to current data connection mapping schema instance.
body_excerpt:
Right now MappingSchema.EntityDescriptorCreatedCallback is defined per-MappingSchema instance, which makes it non-trivial to configure, because user should attach callback to current data connection mapping schema instance.

Attaching it to DefaultSchema will not work, because it is not used directly - we copy schemas from it to actual mapping schema, used by connection in MappingSchema constructor.

There are several ways to fix it:
- make callback application-wide static, so it will be called …

title: Purpose of generic argument in IMetadataReader.GetAttributes<T>
summary: Is there any puprpose in the generic attributte in IMetadataReader.GetAttributes<T> after the attriutes refactoring? Unless I missed something the framework internally only calls IMetadataReader.GetAttributes<MappingAttribute> and I don't see IMetadataReader as part of the public...
body_excerpt: Is there any puprpose in the generic attributte in IMetadataReader.GetAttributes<T> after the attriutes refactoring? Unless I missed something the framework internally only calls IMetadataReader.GetAttributes<MappingAttribute> and I don't see IMetadataReader as part of the public API. Even so it's a minor breaking change, the consumer can simply filter the results with. This would simplify the implementation of provider implementations and remove a lot of type checking.

title: InsertWithOutput inside CTE to use the INSERT RETURNING in a subsequent query
summary: Hello!
body_excerpt:
Hello!

PostgreSQL (maybe other databases as well) supports using the INSERT RETURNING in a subquery inside a CTE to make a subsequent SELECT query based on this.

Sample query:
```sql
WITH INSERT_CTE as (INSERT INTO items (name) VALUES ('test') RETURNING generated_column)
SELECT generated_column FROM INSERT_CTE c
INNER JOIN othertable o ON c.generated_column = o.smth;
```

Would it be possible to make InsertWithOutput and other similar APIs compatible with that?

title: Use COALESCE instead of NVL for nested ??
summary: This is not a bug, everything works fine. It's a small SQL codegen improvement.
body_excerpt:
This is not a bug, everything works fine. It's a small SQL codegen improvement.

linq2db uses `nvl` to translate `??`, which is nice because it's shorter than SQL standard `coalesce`.

The trick with `nvl` is that it only accepts 2 parameters, whereas `coalesce` accepts many.

In our codebase, we have a query that computes an error message based on a list of checks, it looks like this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3832820/137727050-7c7ec386-00b1-4a30-b4cc-522e93636f35.png)

title: Invalid Oracle SQL for boolean IN clause
summary: Assuming column `Boolean` is declare as a `bool`, the following predicate:
body_excerpt:
Assuming column `Boolean` is declare as a `bool`, the following predicate:
```csharp
var list = new List<bool> { true };
var query = db.GetTable<X>().Where(x => list.Contains(x.Boolean));
```
Compiles to the following invalid SQL: 
```sql
WHERE (x.Boolean IS NULL) IN (1)
```
A better translation would be:
```sql
WHERE x.Boolean IN (1)
```

### Environment details
linq2db version: 3.5.0
Database Server: Oracle 19
Database Provider: Oracle.ManagedProvider
Operating system: Win 10
.NET Framework: .…

title: I think the `DataOptions` configuration methods are wrong
summary: I've been trying to configure `BulkCopyTimeout` for the last like 2 hours, and I have noticed the helper methods like `UseBulkCopyTimeout` are actually pure, as in they don't affect `options`, but they aren't marked as such. They all call into `WithOptions`, which is pure, but th...
body_excerpt:
I've been trying to configure `BulkCopyTimeout` for the last like 2 hours, and I have noticed the helper methods like `UseBulkCopyTimeout` are actually pure, as in they don't affect `options`, but they aren't marked as such. They all call into `WithOptions`, which is pure, but they never reset `Options`. I guess that's fine, as long as they are marked as pure.

I guess there isn't any way to update the options after context creation? Like, it's fine, it just tripped me up considerably.

title: Wrong dates with changed culture
summary: In the previous versions of linq2db, CurrentCulture didn't affect datetime values ​​stored in the database. For example, when we worked with Persian calendar dates in our web application, linq2db saved them (datetimes) as a Gregorian calendar values (InvariantCulture) to database...
body_excerpt:
In the previous versions of linq2db, CurrentCulture didn't affect datetime values ​​stored in the database. For example, when we worked with Persian calendar dates in our web application, linq2db saved them (datetimes) as a Gregorian calendar values (InvariantCulture) to databases (not matter of the current culture).

But in the latest version of linq2db (version 5.3.2) this behaviour changed and now it causes issues. Now linq2db started saving dates in current culture. So if your web applicatio…

title: Ensure primary constructor parameters supported as parameters by all providers
summary: We generate parameter names (and aliases) based on identifiers in user code. For a long time we had a good trust in them as compiler will prevent users from using bad names and we didn't cared about "non-conventional" names generation using metadata.
body_excerpt:
We generate parameter names (and aliases) based on identifiers in user code. For a long time we had a good trust in them as compiler will prevent users from using bad names and we didn't cared about "non-conventional" names generation using metadata.

All changed with C# 12 and primary constructor parameters. With this feature compiler leaks real backing field names to expressions and e.g. it result in bad parameter names like `@<myvalue>`.

We need to add test for such case and ensure all provi…

title: ToListAsync() thread problems with LoadWith() queries
summary: `ToListAsync()` has a thread problem when called with a non `ExpressionQuery<>` source such as `LoadWithQueryable<>`.
body_excerpt:
`ToListAsync()` has a thread problem when called with a non `ExpressionQuery<>` source such as `LoadWithQueryable<>`.
`db.MyTable.Where(...).LoadWith(...).ToListAsync()` can crash as the DB connection is used in a thread pool task.
`db.MyTable.LoadWith(...).Where(...).ToListAsync()` works however as the source is a `ExpressionQuery<>` in that case.

```
You can get various thread problem related crashes, such as:

Cannot access a disposed object.
Object name: 'SQLiteCommand'.
at System.Data.SQLi…

body_excerpt:
See https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/discussions/4324#discussioncomment-7382490

We should:
- use `DataOptions<TContextImplementation>`
- probe and register `DataOptions<TContext>` as fallback for backward compatibility if `DataOptions<TContextImplementation>` constructor missing

body_excerpt:
We should consider renaming existing `linq2db.AspNet` package to something like `linq2db.Configuration` as existing name doesn't correctly reflect package functionality.
Because nuget doesn't have "rename" concept, we will need to obsolete old package and publish newer versions as new package with new name.

title: Question: Are the Nullable/NotNull attributes still needed in C#8 with Nullable Reference Types?
summary: I see that the Nullable and NotNull attributes are needed for marking class types (mainly string) where we previously had no way to know if they were meant to be nullable or not. But now in C#8, you can just set the type as `string?` and the compiler will mark it with a Nullable ...
body_excerpt:
I see that the Nullable and NotNull attributes are needed for marking class types (mainly string) where we previously had no way to know if they were meant to be nullable or not. But now in C#8, you can just set the type as `string?` and the compiler will mark it with a Nullable attribute.

So, my question is, is explicitly setting Nullable/NotNull still required for LinqToDb when using it in C# 8 or 9?

I tried looking in the documentation and the issues, but I couldn't find any relevant info.

title: F# option types are not supported
summary: FSharp has the concept of `Option` types, which can hold the value of `None|Some<T>`. They make handling values that can be null very easy:
body_excerpt:
FSharp has the concept of `Option` types, which can hold the value of `None|Some<T>`. They make handling values that can be null very easy:

``` f#
let f = fun (x : string option) -> 
    match x with 
    | None -> "None"
    | Some(x) -> x

f(Some("foo")) //-> "foo"
f(None) //-> "None"
```

What I am thinking is adding support for colums being typed as `Option<T>`, and they are then implictly treated as a `<Nullable>` column. If the value is null in the database, they are set to `None`, if its…

title: Bad boolean translation in Oracle analytic functions (OVER (ORDER BY))
summary: Linq2db usually evaluates boolean expressions such as `field == 1` correctly but I found a corner case where it did not: in the order by part of an analytical function.
body_excerpt:
Linq2db usually evaluates boolean expressions such as `field == 1` correctly but I found a corner case where it did not: in the order by part of an analytical function.

I tried this:
```csharp
Sql.Ext.Rank().Over().OrderBy(x.Level == 1).ToValue()
```
And it generated this invalid SQL -- at least for Oracle that doesn't have boolean types in SQL:
```sql
rank() over (order by (x.level = 1))
```

I worked around the problem by forcing a correct translation with:
```csharp
Sql.Ext.Rank().Over().Ord…

Hi guys,

I have a small question regarding the way Linq2Db treats ambient transactions (TransactionScope).

In the example (see below), if the first query runs before an instance of transaction scope is created, then the ambient transaction will be ignored. However, if the first query runs after the transaction scope is created, then the transaction will be respected.

title: Multiple Levels of Abstract Inheritance Results in Failed Reads
summary: While refactoring our project to use polymorphism, we have found that Linq2DB's inheritance mapping falls apart when the tree of abstract classes becomes three deep. The mapping works fine if there is a base abstract class and a single layer of derived types.
body_excerpt:
While refactoring our project to use polymorphism, we have found that Linq2DB's inheritance mapping falls apart when the tree of abstract classes becomes three deep. The mapping works fine if there is a base abstract class and a single layer of derived types.

### tl;dr:
When mapping to a class tree with two or more levels of abstract classes:
* Reads and Inserts made from concrete classes behave as expected
* Reads using base types fail
* Inserts of collections of base types fail
* [A project i…

title: [SQL Server] Implement ISNULL optimization
summary: `ISNULL` is a built-in SQL Server function, which calculates parameters only once, `COALESCE` (and `IIF`) is a "macro", converted to `CASE` statement.
body_excerpt:
`ISNULL` is a built-in SQL Server function, which calculates parameters only once, `COALESCE` (and `IIF`) is a "macro", converted to `CASE` statement.
For complex parameters like subqueries `CASE` leads to double evaluation of parameters.

We already have tests that demonstate this behavior:
- `SelectTests.Coalesce4` (subquery)
- `SelectTests.Coalesce5` (subquery)
- `GroupByTests.GroupByExpression` (aggregate)

title: Async API Guidelines Design
summary: It is not like I think it will be redundant, but I agree that we should postpone it for now. You unintentionally rised issue with our async API surface - it is not systematic. Earliest versions of async API provide ToList/ToArray methods, later we started to use Task<IEnumerable>...
body_excerpt:
It is not like I think it will be redundant, but I agree that we should postpone it for now. You unintentionally rised issue with our async API surface - it is not systematic. Earliest versions of async API provide ToList/ToArray methods, later we started to use Task<IEnumerable> but probably IAsyncEnumerable is correct answer here. Also cancellation token use differ between methods.

We should take timeout to think and decide on async API guidelines. After that we will review/refactor existing …

title: Exception when specific Sql.AssociationAlias is set
summary: Hi,
body_excerpt:
Hi,

Usually most of us set `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias` to `null` or just `"{0}"`, whatever. That works fine producing query with association for example `[Table].[Field]`.

However when `AssociationAlias` is set for example to `"db].[{0}";` we receive exception because produced query is `[db].[Table].[Field]`.

I know... Nobody will set such a fancy association alias as this :) So you can close this issue if you think it is irrelevant bug.

title: Alternative Update.Set() api?
summary: An idea that crossed my mind when looking at some updates that use `Sql.Row`:
body_excerpt:
An idea that crossed my mind when looking at some updates that use `Sql.Row`:

The following is an easier to use API:
```
Set<T>(Expression<Func<T, IQueryable<T>> setter)
```

It works like a partial `.Update(setter)`, for example:
```csharp
db.Item
  .Set(i => from o in Order
            where o.ItemId == i.Id
            select new Item { Stock = Sql.Ext.Count() })
  .Update();
```

The builder for this `Set` could immediately convert it into `.Set(i => i.Stock, i => query.Select(i => Sql.Ext.…

create table if not exists type_no_nullable_parent

);

The generated code would be:

Specifically this is about the equality `ExprExpr =` handling.

Adding properties to entity with explicit configuration doesn't set configuration to properties too:
```cs
mappingBuilder.Entity<SomeEntity>("MyConfiguration")
    // property will have no configuration, when it is expected for it to be "MyConfiguration"
    .Property(e => e.SomeProperty);
```

Not sure yet, but it could be useful also to have move overloads with configuration name, e.g. `Property(configuration)`.

body_excerpt:
My case: two data contexts are generated as partial classes to be merged into one (for cross database queries). As now two `partial void InitDataContext();` exists I get compilation error. Need to manually remove it from generation, deal with messed up formatting and git diffs as autoformatting is applied on save. And newbies can be confused after regeneration.
So not big deal, but annoying.
Didn't found any option to disable it.

title: Setting `BulkCopyOptions`
summary: There doesn't seem to be a way to set `BulkCopyTimeout` for example, on the whole context, rather than per operation, non-globally. The only way to configure it seems to be setting a value for `LinqToDBOptionsExtension` on EF Core's `IDbContextOptions`, which seems to be the resp...
body_excerpt:
There doesn't seem to be a way to set `BulkCopyTimeout` for example, on the whole context, rather than per operation, non-globally. The only way to configure it seems to be setting a value for `LinqToDBOptionsExtension` on EF Core's `IDbContextOptions`, which seems to be the responsibility of `UseLinqToDB`.
Anyway, I suggest adding a configuration parameter to `CreateLinqToDbContext` for `DataOptions` to make configuration like that possible.

body_excerpt:
## Problem
linq2db implementation of Power only accepts doubles and not decimals, unlike all other Math functions such Log or Round.

`Sql.Power`: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/Sql/Sql.cs#L1339
`Math.Pow`: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/Linq/Expressions.cs#L1014

## Consequences
This can lead to subtle inaccuracies when using constants.
For example, consider expression `x * Sql.Power(10, 2)` when field `x` is `8.7345`:

In Oracle, …

I use the library to interact with ClickHouse (Clickhouse.Client provider). If I call the deletion of records, it is implemented through the following command: 
`ALTER TABLE table DELETE WHERE filter_expr`
However, Clickhouse has long been able to perform lightweight deletions ([The Lightweight DELETE Statement](https://clickhouse.com/docs/en/sql-reference/statements/delete)):
`DELETE FROM table WHERE filter_expr`
Will the ability to do such deletion be implemented in the linq2db?

title: Excessive OR brackets
summary: Suppose you have this long chain of `&&`:
body_excerpt:
Suppose you have this long chain of `&&`:
```csharp
query.Where(x => x.N > 10 && x.N > 11 && x.N > 12 && x.N > 13 && x.N > 14)
```

Linqtodb generates the following, nice, SQL:
```sql
WHERE
	x.N > 10 AND
	x.N > 11 AND
	x.N > 12 AND
	x.N > 13 AND
	x.N > 14
```

But if you have a chain of `||` such as: 
```csharp
query.Where(x => x.N > 10 || x.N > 11 || x.N > 12 || x.N > 13 || x.N > 14)
```
Then it generates:
```sql
WHERE
	(((x.N > 10 OR x.N > 11) OR (x.N > 12 OR x.N > 13)) OR x.N > 14)
```

Obvio…

summary: We are upgrading from old version and we see that Linq2DB now treats empty string/null differently.
body_excerpt:
We are upgrading from old version and we see that Linq2DB now treats empty string/null differently.

We used to have enums mapped like this.

```
public enum PaymentMethod
{
	[MapValue("")]  <-- note this
	Unknown,
	
	[MapValue("C"]
	Cheque,

	[MapValue("E")]
	EFT
}
```

Given query

```
from db.Table x
where x.PaymentMethod != PaymentMethod.Unknown
select x
```

Old version would produce the following

```
SELECT
...
FROM TABLE x
WHERE x.PAYMENTMETHOD IS NOT NULL
```

New version produces

when you use in a query function like **!string.IsNullOrEmpty** it is converted to SQL like

FROM

and according to [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/len-transact-sql?view=sql-server-ver16] **LEN** Returns the number of characters of the specified string expression, **excluding trailing spaces**. It means that string of space characters will not m…

title: Update CTE support for various providers
summary: Made some tests with failing providers:
body_excerpt:
Made some tests with failing providers:
- SAP HANA: supports CTE in source query (CTE should be moved inside source clause as part of source query) but linq2db doesn't support CTE for SAP HANA right now
- Oracle: also supports cte if it is a part of source query. Also oracle doesn't like `_` as table alias
- DB2: version 11.5 we use on CI declares CTE support in target so it makes sense to test it (note that syntax is differ from sql server: https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/db2/11.5?topic=statements-…

title: OracleBulkCopy default implementation
summary: The Oracle provider has, in my opinion, strange defaults when it comes to bulk copy.
body_excerpt:
The Oracle provider has, in my opinion, strange defaults when it comes to bulk copy.

**1. It defaults to multiple rows.**

Wouldn't it be better to check if `OracleBulkCopy` is available? 
Unfortunately it's available only in the native provider (not classic-managed, nor core-managed) and Oracle seems in no hurry to fix that. 
But when using native, isn't that the best option?

**2. Multiple rows defaults to INSERT ALL**

With Oracle, isn't INSERT INTO actually a better choice?
It uses array pa…

body_excerpt:
Linq2db throws if I use a Postgres integer array. Since there is the Overlaps method I assume it should be supported. Am I missing a conversion. Is using  a `.HasConversion` or `.HasConversionFunc` not enough?

```c#
// of type StatusType[] (enum)

title: DDL [drop table] causes MySql transactions to fail to roll back
Remove `timestamp` "type" special handling in SQLite schema provider
summary: See https://forum.linqpad.net/discussion/3128/cant-insert-records-with-timestamp-fields-in-sqlite#latest
body_excerpt:
See https://forum.linqpad.net/discussion/3128/cant-insert-records-with-timestamp-fields-in-sqlite#latest

For some unknown reason we treat column with type name `timespan` as autogenerated field. This doesn't make sense as:
- type names in SQLite means nothing
- there is no autogenerated columns in SQLite

We should remove this code https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/DataProvider/SQLite/SQLiteSchemaProvider.cs#L117-L118

title: RetryPolicy and OnTraceConnection error handling does not work properly in UPDATE statements with OUTPUT clauses
summary: Our application uses a DataConnection with an IRetryPolicy that maps SqlExceptions to custom exceptions defined by us, and an OnTraceConnection handler (with TraceSwitchConnection.Level = Error) to log SqlExceptions.
body_excerpt:
### Description

Our application uses a DataConnection with an IRetryPolicy that maps SqlExceptions to custom exceptions defined by us, and an OnTraceConnection handler (with TraceSwitchConnection.Level = Error) to log SqlExceptions.

In almost all cases, this works well, but when UPDATE statements with OUTPUT clauses cause a database exception (e.g. due to the update violating a unique constraint), these error-handling mechanisms fail.

title: Test and fix string.Length translation for border cases
summary: We need to add tests than ensure we translate string.Length properly at least for following cases:
body_excerpt:
We need to add tests than ensure we translate string.Length properly at least for following cases:

- string with leading/trailing spaces
- varchar strings (ascii-only characters)
- nvarchar with fixed-size collation with non-ascii characters for supported databases
- nvarchar with utf8 collation with non-ascii characters for supported databases

All failing cases should be fixed.

title: 'Unnest' is server-side method.
summary: Hi! Can you help me. How i can use Unnest with fluent notation?
body_excerpt:
Hi! Can you help me. How i can use Unnest with fluent notation?
This code is working
```
var resourcesQuery =
    from r in db.Resources
    from i in db.Unnest(ids)
    where r.Id == i
    select r;
```
but
```
var resourcesQuery = db
    .Unnest(ids)
    .InnerJoin(db.Resources, (x, y) => y.Id == x, (_, y) => y);
```
thrown exception `LinqToDB.Linq.LinqException : 'Unnest' is server-side method.`

title: Oracle Database CHAR(n) Column Parameter Error with VARCHAR2(n) in C# Data Access Layer
summary: When interacting with Oracle database columns defined as `CHAR(n)` from a C# application, there is an inconsistency in how the parameters are handled. Specifically, when a column is defined as `CHAR(6)` in the database, passing a `VARCHAR2(5)` parameter fails with an error becaus...
body_excerpt:
### Description

When interacting with Oracle database columns defined as `CHAR(n)` from a C# application, there is an inconsistency in how the parameters are handled. Specifically, when a column is defined as `CHAR(6)` in the database, passing a `VARCHAR2(5)` parameter fails with an error because the lengths do not match. This issue arises because `CHAR` columns in Oracle are fixed-length and require padding to match the defined length.

For example:

title: Arbitrarily nested LoadWith's no longer fully work in using Where clauses that also use the same properties before
summary: It seems arbitrarily nested `LoadWith`'s no longer work in all previous cases.
body_excerpt:
### Description

It seems arbitrarily nested `LoadWith`'s no longer work in all previous cases.
Possibly continuation of #4797 ?

It seems that when using both `Where` and `LoadWith`, in 6.0 ordering matters, if the `Where` touches the same properties as the `LoadWith`.
Example:

```cs
// worked before, but no only seems to allow 1-level deep LoadWith
db.GetTable<Person>()
  .Where(p => p.Children.Any(c => c.IsHungry))
  .LoadWith(
    p => p.Children,
    child => child.LoadWith(c => c.Friends)…

title: SQLite: "Integer Primary Key" columns fail to auto increment when not flagged as autoincrement (Yes, it's really a bug)
summary: SQLite tables have a hidden "ROW ID" column. When a table has an "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY" then that column _might_ become an alias for ROW ID. These columns act differently to a regular INTEGER column, or to a BIGINT PRIMARY KEY column (despite BIGINT being otherwise synonymous with...
body_excerpt:
SQLite tables have a hidden "ROW ID" column. When a table has an "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY" then that column _might_ become an alias for ROW ID. These columns act differently to a regular INTEGER column, or to a BIGINT PRIMARY KEY column (despite BIGINT being otherwise synonymous with INTEGER). 

These columns act something like an auto-increment column, so that when it's left empty or NULL during an insert, SQLite gives it a value automatically. It differs from a "true" autoincrementing field in tha…

Why BulkCopy run without parameters?

Simple insert generates SQL with parameters:

> db.Insert(new SubmissionFile() { Status = "Uploaded2"});

`INSERT INTO submission_files
(
	status,
	filename,
	created,
	created_by
)
VALUES
(
	:Status,
	:Filename,
	:Created,
	:CreatedBy
)`

But BulkCopy generates SQL without parameters:
>db.BulkCopy<SubmissionFile>(new List<SubmissionFile>()
{
	new SubmissionFile() { Status = "Uploaded" },
	new SubmissionFile() { Status = "Uploaded2"}
});

`INSERT INTO submis…

### Description

Implementation of remote/non-remote baselines comparison found several cases where we generate different SQL. Some of them were fixed, others are tracked by this issue.

- [PostgreSQL] `TestTypesInsertByMerge()` test deduplicate enumerable source parameters, but direct call - don't. Caused by different call sites to `SqlValuesTable.BuildRows(...)`

title: [Task] Implement baselines comparison for direct and remote context testruns
summary: Test baselines collector should compare test baselines from normal and remote context runs and:
body_excerpt:
Test baselines collector should compare test baselines from normal and remote context runs and:
- normalize baselines for comparison (like removing async marker, generated for remote context always)
- generate error if baselines doesn't match
- save only one baseline file (reduce disk space use)

This will allow:
- detect implementation issues for remote context when it generates different sql
- help with disk space issue on CI we have from time to time

summary: SQL Server introduced a [`CONCAT()`][1] function in MSSQLServer 2012.  This function returns a non-null `varchar(..)` or `nvarchar(..)` string that represents the concatenation of all of the input parameters, no matter what type or value those parameters are as long as they can b...
body_excerpt:
SQL Server introduced a [`CONCAT()`][1] function in MSSQLServer 2012.  This function returns a non-null `varchar(..)` or `nvarchar(..)` string that represents the concatenation of all of the input parameters, no matter what type or value those parameters are as long as they can be cast to `varchar(..)` or `nvarchar(..)`.  `NULL` values are converted to 0-length `varchar(1)`, everything else is converted to the appropriate text value.
