title: `MergeWithOutputAsync` with a `Tuple.Create(…)` expression generates an empty `OUTPUT` clause in `6.0.0-rc.3`
summary: `linq2db` 6 generates an empty `OUTPUT` clause from a `Tuple.Create(…)` expression passed to `MergeWithOutputAsync`, which errors out as malformed SQL code, while `linq2db` 5 handles this correctly.
body_excerpt:
### Description

`linq2db` 6 generates an empty `OUTPUT` clause from a `Tuple.Create(…)` expression passed to `MergeWithOutputAsync`, which errors out as malformed SQL code, while `linq2db` 5 handles this correctly.

Workaround is to use an anonymous type instead, and work around the resulting inability to use a destructuring assignment for the output.

CC @coudy.

### Reproduction Steps

Reproduction code with workarounds: https://github.com/megaslice/P1-linq2db-repros/blob/master/SpecificRepro…

title: Full-text search (SQL Server) of multiple joined entire tables broken in `6.0.0-rc.3`
summary: A sequence of `Sql.Ext.SqlServer().Contains(…)` calls joined with `||`, where each references an entire (joined) table, fails in linq2db `6.0.0-rc.3` but succeeds in linq2db 5.  Searches of inner-joined tables get omitted from the query, while those of left-joined tables error ou...
body_excerpt:
### Description

A sequence of `Sql.Ext.SqlServer().Contains(…)` calls joined with `||`, where each references an entire (joined) table, fails in linq2db `6.0.0-rc.3` but succeeds in linq2db 5.  Searches of inner-joined tables get omitted from the query, while those of left-joined tables error out and fail to generate SQL.

Workaround is to exhaustively pass in all of the full-text indexed columns instead of passing in the table object.

CC @coudy.

title: Linq2db 6.0.0 sends json parameters as json for unsupported SQL Server versions
summary: After upgrading to linq2db 6.0.0 some of our queries started to fail on SQL Server. The issue is that because we support multiple databases (SQL Server and PostgreSQL) we use `DataType.Json` for json parameters, where it's a native json column for PostgreSQL and nvarchar(max) for...
body_excerpt:
### Description

After upgrading to linq2db 6.0.0 some of our queries started to fail on SQL Server. The issue is that because we support multiple databases (SQL Server and PostgreSQL) we use `DataType.Json` for json parameters, where it's a native json column for PostgreSQL and nvarchar(max) for SQL Server. It seems like starting with 6.0.0 linq2db always uses the native json type for SQL Server. This is a bit surprising, given that we explicitly specify that the version of SQL Server is 2012, …

title: Multi-row INSERT with ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE support?
summary: It seems that the MERGE API from ANSI 2003 is not part of MySQL, and Linq2db wants to use existing features only. So the MERGE API relies directly on the MERGE statement as we can read here: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/issues/1596
body_excerpt:
### Description


It seems that the MERGE API from ANSI 2003 is not part of MySQL, and Linq2db wants to use existing features only. So the MERGE API relies directly on the MERGE statement as we can read here: https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/issues/1596

In such case it would be nice to allow MySQL features separately as part of another call. For example, `Insert` method can accept multiple objects and produce something like this (with options of course):

```SQL
INSERT INTO MyEntity (Id, Name…

In the EF in EntityTypeBuilder it is done as follows:

`builder.HasBaseType((Type)null)`

In .net 6 linq2db.EntityFrameworkCore worked normally. But in .net 8 an error appeared "Inheritance Discriminator is not defined.."

The reason is that EFCoreMetadataReader contains the condition

title: PostgreSQL regression in 6.0.0: correlated subquery over FromSql("unnest(...)") loses LATERAL correlation and generates a."Value" > "Int32"
summary: After upgrading linq2db from 5.1.4.9 to 6.0.0, a query that uses an ExpressionMethod wrapper around FromSql("unnest(...)") and a correlated Take(1) subquery starts generating invalid SQL for PostgreSQL.
body_excerpt:
### Description

### Summary

After upgrading linq2db from 5.1.4.9 to 6.0.0, a query that uses an ExpressionMethod wrapper around FromSql("unnest(...)") and a correlated Take(1) subquery starts generating invalid SQL for PostgreSQL.

In 5.1.4.9 the SQL:
- uses a LATERAL join and correctly correlates the subquery with the unnest() element (x."Value" > i.*).

In 6.0.0 the generated SQL:
- drops LATERAL semantics (uses plain CROSS JOIN (...)),
- replaces the correlated reference to the unnest eleme…

This is not and issue, just inconsistent behavior, discovered in https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/pull/5291

We need:
- implement SqlConcat AST element and migrate/map all concat methods/operations to it
- review type conversion generation for string concat operators/functions (`+`, `||` or `concat(...)`) to generate only needed conversions
- update `TestCase2` test asserts to ensure there is no `WHERE` clause

title: Skip & Take works without OrderBy
summary: When doing simple windowing paging one might use `.Skip(...).Take(...)` which exists similarly underneath in most SQL dialects.
body_excerpt:
### Description

When doing simple windowing paging one might use `.Skip(...).Take(...)` which exists similarly underneath in most SQL dialects.
TSQL has `offset ... rows fetch next ... rows only`
_However_ this only works in combination with `order by`.
linq2db generates the following SQL which _works_ but can lead to unexpected results.
```sql
-- SqlServer.2008
DECLARE @skip Int -- Int32
SET     @skip = 0
DECLARE @take Int -- Int32
SET     @take = 100

SELECT
        [t2].[Id],
        [t2].[c…

title: Fluent mapping API refactoring
summary: Currently fluent mapping builders (entity and property builders) are overloaded with logic for some specific mapping attributes (e.g. `ColumnAttribute` or `TableAttribute`). We should:
body_excerpt:
Currently fluent mapping builders (entity and property builders) are overloaded with logic for some specific mapping attributes (e.g. `ColumnAttribute` or `TableAttribute`). We should:
- move support for specific mapping attribute to builder extensions
- add more extensions for existing mapping atributes

title: Allow BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout to work with Npgsql COPY operations
summary: Currently, `BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout` doesn't work for npgsql copy operations even though the corresponding property (`Timeout`) has been there since Npgsql 5 (which is around 5 years old). Could you consider allowing `BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout` to set `Timeout` prop...
body_excerpt:
### Description

Currently, `BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout` doesn't work for npgsql copy operations even though the corresponding property (`Timeout`) has been there since Npgsql 5 (which is around 5 years old). Could you consider allowing `BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout` to set `Timeout` property?

title: CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters does not work in v6
summary: `LikeSqlExceptParameters` behaves just like `LikeClr`, which is wrong.
body_excerpt:
### Description

`LikeSqlExceptParameters` behaves just like `LikeClr`, which is wrong.

Reminder of the intended behavior:
- `LikeClr`: emulates CLR behavior by adding `IS NULL` clauses to all comparisons.
- `LikeSql`: converts straight to SQL 1:1, so the runtime behavior is different (as SQL `NULL` values never compare as true). Only exception is (in-)equality with literal `null` or `default`: `x == null` translates to `x is null`.
- `LikeSqlExceptParameters`: applies `LikeSql` logic to any cl…

body_excerpt:
### Description

A CTE in 6.x removes columns in CTE `SELECT`s.

In linq2db **5.4.1** the columns of the CTE C# `Select(...)` calls where also visible in the SQL text (necessarily so).
In linq2db 6.2.1 (all of 6.x (maybe not preview)) only some (in my case 1 of 4) columns are in the SQL text - required columns are removed.

Last time I debugged (months ago) I saw that the new `RemoveUnusedColumnsVisitor` tried to remove columns, but there was a quasi out-of-bounds acces because one array had a s…

title: PostgreSQL CTE MATERIALIZED
summary: Starting with PostgreSql 12 it [supports](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/12/queries-with.html) special keywords `MATERIALIZED` and `NOT MATERIALIZED` to rule CTE materialization inside query.
body_excerpt:
### Description

Starting with PostgreSql 12 it [supports](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/12/queries-with.html) special keywords `MATERIALIZED` and `NOT MATERIALIZED` to rule CTE materialization inside query.

Seems like current Linq2Db API doesn't support this and it is very odd, because PG released this feature **7 years ago** (PG 12 published in 2019)...

I tried to find workaround with current hints implementation, but no success:

```cs

title: Firebird 6: use native `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` / `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` instead of `EXECUTE BLOCK` workaround
summary: For the Firebird 6 dialect (`FirebirdVersion.v6`), linq2db should drop the legacy `EXECUTE BLOCK` + `rdb$relations` lookup workaround for `CreateIfNotExists` / `DropIfExists` in favor of the native `IF [NOT] EXISTS` clauses added in Firebird 6.
body_excerpt:
### Summary

For the Firebird 6 dialect (`FirebirdVersion.v6`), linq2db should drop the legacy `EXECUTE BLOCK` + `rdb$relations` lookup workaround for `CreateIfNotExists` / `DropIfExists` in favor of the native `IF [NOT] EXISTS` clauses added in Firebird 6.

### Background

Firebird 6 introduces native `IF EXISTS` / `IF NOT EXISTS` support for `DROP` and `CREATE` statements across many DDL object types (`TABLE`, `VIEW`, `INDEX`, `TRIGGER`, `PROCEDURE`, `FUNCTION`, `SEQUENCE`/`GENERATOR`, `EXCEPT…

summary: `FromSqlScalar<T>(FormattableString)` — and therefore `PostgreSQLExtensions.Unnest<T>(this IDataContext, T[])` which internally [delegates to it](https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/DataProvider/PostgreSQL/PostgreSQLExtensions.cs) — compiles a fresh map...
body_excerpt:
### Description

### Summary

`FromSqlScalar<T>(FormattableString)` — and therefore `PostgreSQLExtensions.Unnest<T>(this IDataContext, T[])` which internally [delegates to it](https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/DataProvider/PostgreSQL/PostgreSQLExtensions.cs) — compiles a fresh mapper on every call on linq2db 6.x instead of hitting the compiled-query cache. Per-call time goes from ~0.5 ms (5.4.1.9) to ~3 ms (6.0.0+), a 6× regression. Generated SQL is unchanged semanti…

title: Regression v6: bad translation of DateTimeOffset.Now
summary: Before v6, when `DateTimeOffset.Now` appeared inside queries it was usually replaced by a constant parameter.
body_excerpt:
### Description

Before v6, when `DateTimeOffset.Now` appeared inside queries it was usually replaced by a constant parameter.

Now it's translated into SQL, which is ok, except the translation is incorrect in Oracle.

See repro for more details but in the query I see `sys_extract_utc(systimestamp)`. 
The problem is that `sys_extract_utc` looses all TZ infos, it only gets a time (in UTC).
If you insert this into a `timestamp with timezone` column then Oracle will assume the tz-less date is a loc…

title: Regression v6: can't assign a column with converter inside Sql.Row
summary: It looks like linq2db has a hard-coded check to refuse updating a column in a `Sql.Row` if that column has a value converter.
body_excerpt:
### Description

It looks like linq2db has a hard-coded check to refuse updating a column in a `Sql.Row` if that column has a value converter.

I think linq2db should just do the update without value conversion. After all, in terms of "c# model vs sql model" the update goes from sql value to sql model, so no conversion required.
**It used to work like this before v6.**

This is a blocker issue because short of removing the value converter (which has big implications on the rest of the codebase),…

title: Multiple result set query builder
summary: Is there any helper to assemble a query string for `QueryMultiple`?
body_excerpt:
### Description

Is there any helper to assemble a query string for `QueryMultiple`? 
Now, we use `ToSqlQuery`, join queries by ";" and merge parameters. 
However, this has some caveats. For example, some queries could contain parameters with the same name, but different values. 

This problem could be solved by adding `string? ParameterNamesPostfix` to `SqlGenerationOptions`. 
But it would be nice to have a more fancy API for building multiple result set queries. I can propose one.

### Description

When using `UPDATE` query with a `ServerSideOnly` function and `ValueConverter` on a PostgreSQL JSONB column, the query fails with "The LINQ expression could not be converted to SQL" error.

The issue occurs when:
1. Entity property has a `ValueConverter` configured (e.g., JSON serialization)
2. UPDATE query uses a `ServerSideOnly` function to transform the value and returns JSONB
3. The function is called within the `.Set()` expression to update JSONB-type column

Execute the following query:
